rootCauseAnalysis.summary: A 'deny-all-ingress' NetworkPolicy (injected by kubernaut-demo at 16:55:32) blocks all ingress traffic in namespace demo-netpol-2 via an empty podSelector with policyTypes=[Ingress] and no allow rules. This causes the traffic-gen readiness probe (curl to web-frontend:8080/healthz) to time out, leaving the traffic-gen pod stuck in NotReady state and the deployment reporting 0 available replicas despite 1 running.
contributingFactors: NetworkPolicy 'deny-all-ingress' applied with empty podSelector (affects ALL pods in namespace) and policyTypes=[Ingress] with no allow rules — blocks all inbound traffic
traffic-gen readiness probe 'curl -sf -o /dev/null -m 3 http://web-frontend.demo-netpol-2:8080/healthz' times out because ingress to web-frontend on port 8080 is denied by the policy
22 consecutive probe failures confirmed in pod events ('command timed out after 1s') between 16:55:33 and 16:57:12
Pod logs show clear transition: probe=success before NetworkPolicy injection at 16:55:32, then persistent probe=failure immediately after
NetworkPolicy was injected by 'kubernaut-demo' (label: injected-by=kubernaut-demo) exactly ~16s after pod start, matching the onset of failures
Deployment status: replicas=1, unavailableReplicas=1, Available=False with reason MinimumReplicasUnavailable

kaResponse.analysis: A 'deny-all-ingress' NetworkPolicy (injected by kubernaut-demo at 16:55:32) blocks all ingress traffic in namespace demo-netpol-2 via an empty podSelector with policyTypes=[Ingress] and no allow rules. This causes the traffic-gen readiness probe (curl to web-frontend:8080/healthz) to time out, leaving the traffic-gen pod stuck in NotReady state and the deployment reporting 0 available replicas despite 1 running.
